issues.issueMessage: Sidecar key defined in /task-FacePerception_eeg.json overrides previous value ([object Object]) from /sub-002/ses-1/eeg/sub-002_ses-1_task-FacePerception_run-1_eeg.json
Field description: Used to specify provenance of the dataset.

Field description: Used to specify the locations and relevant attributes of all source datasets (BIDS or not).
Valid keys in each object include `"URL"`, `"DOI"` (see
[URI](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#uniform-resource-indicator)), and
`"Version"` with
[string](https://www.w3schools.com/js/js_json_datatypes.asp)
values.

Field description: Name of the task.
No two tasks should have the same name.
The task label included in the filename MAY be derived from this `"TaskName"` field
by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.
Field description: Text of the instructions given to participants before the recording.
Field description: Longer description of the task.

Field description: [URI](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#uniform-resource-indicator)
of the corresponding [Cognitive Atlas](https://www.cognitiveatlas.org/)
Task term.

Field description: [URI](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#uniform-resource-indicator)
of the corresponding [CogPO](http://www.cogpo.org/) term.

Field description: The name of the institution in charge of the equipment that produced the
measurements.

Field description: The address of the institution in charge of the equipment that produced the
measurements.

Field description: The department in the institution in charge of the equipment that produced
the measurements.

Field description: Name of the task.
No two tasks should have the same name.
The task label included in the filename MAY be derived from this `"TaskName"` field
by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.
Format "string" must be number
Sidecar key defined in /task-FacePerception_eeg.json overrides previous value ([object Object]) from /sub-002/ses-1/eeg/sub-002_ses-1_task-FacePerception_run-2_eeg.json
Sidecar key defined in /task-FacePerception_eeg.json overrides previous value ([object Object]) from /sub-002/ses-1/eeg/sub-002_ses-1_task-FacePerception_run-3_eeg.json
Field description: Manufacturer of the equipment that produced the measurements.
Field description: Manufacturer's model name of the equipment that produced the measurements.

Field description: Manufacturer's designation of software version of the equipment that produced
the measurements.

Field description: The serial number of the equipment that produced the measurements.
A pseudonym can also be used to prevent the equipment from being
identifiable, so long as each pseudonym is unique within the dataset.
Field description: Text of the instructions given to participants before the recording.

Field description: [URI](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#uniform-resource-indicator)
of the corresponding [Cognitive Atlas](https://www.cognitiveatlas.org/)
Task term.

Field description: [URI](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#uniform-resource-indicator)
of the corresponding [CogPO](http://www.cogpo.org/) term.

Field description: The department in the institution in charge of the equipment that produced
the measurements.

Field description: Manufacturer's designation of the cap model
(for example, `"actiCAP 64 Ch Standard-2"`).
Field description: Number of miscellaneous channels not covered otherwise.

Field description: Number of channels for digital (binary TTL) triggers or analog equivalents (TTL in volt).
Corresponds to the `TRIG` channel type.
Field description: Circumference of the participant's head, expressed in cm (for example, `58`).

Field description: Object of temporal hardware filters applied, or `"n/a"` if the data is not
available. Each key-value pair in the JSON object is a name of the filter and
an object in which its parameters are defined as key-value pairs.
For example, `{"Highpass RC filter": {"Half amplitude cutoff (Hz)":
0.0159, "Roll-off": "6dB/Octave"}}`.

Field description: Freeform description of the observed subject artifact and its possible cause
(for example, `"Vagus Nerve Stimulator"`, `"non-removable implant"`).
If this field is set to `"n/a"`, it will be interpreted as absence of major
source of artifacts except cardiac and blinks.

Field description: Object containing key-value pairs related to the software used to present
the stimuli during the experiment.

Field description: Institution defined name of the machine that produced the measurements.

Field description: Manufacturer's designation of software version of the equipment that produced
the measurements.

Field description: Nominal field strength of MR magnet in Tesla.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0087](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0087))
`Magnetic Field Strength`.

Field description: Information describing the receiver coil.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 1250](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,1250))
`Receive Coil Name`,
although not all vendors populate that DICOM Tag,
in which case this field can be derived from an appropriate
private DICOM field.

Field description: Information describing the active/selected elements of the receiver coil.
This does not correspond to a tag in the DICOM ontology.
The vendor-defined terminology for active coil elements can go in this field.

Field description: (If used)
A method for reducing the number of independent channels by combining in
analog the signals from multiple coil elements.
There are typically different default modes when using un-accelerated or
accelerated (for example, `"GRAPPA"`, `"SENSE"`) imaging.

Field description: Almost all fMRI studies using phased-array coils use root-sum-of-squares
(rSOS) combination, but other methods exist.
The image reconstruction is changed by the coil combination method
(as for the matrix coil mode above),
so anything non-standard should be reported.
Field description: A general description of the pulse sequence used for the scan.

Field description: Description of the type of data acquired.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0020](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0020))
`Scanning Sequence`.

Field description: Variant of the ScanningSequence.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0021](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0021))
`Sequence Variant`.

Field description: Manufacturer's designation of the sequence name.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0024](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0024))
`Sequence Name`.

Field description: Information beyond pulse sequence type that identifies the specific pulse
sequence used (for example,
`"Standard Siemens Sequence distributed with the VB17 software"`,
`"Siemens WIP ### version #.##,"` or
`"Sequence written by X using a version compiled on MM/DD/YYYY"`).

Field description: Boolean stating if the image saved has been corrected for gradient
nonlinearities by the scanner sequence.

Field description: Type of sequence readout.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0023](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0023))
`MR Acquisition Type`.

Field description: The echo time (TE) for the acquisition, specified in seconds.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0081](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0081))
`Echo Time`
(please note that the DICOM term is in milliseconds not seconds).
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`echo` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#echo).

Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

Field description: Flip angle (FA) for the acquisition, specified in degrees.
Corresponds to: [DICOM Tag 0018, 1314](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,1314))
`Flip Angle`.
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`flip` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#flip).

Field description: Description(s) of the primary antibody used for immunostaining.
Either an [RRID](https://rrid.site) or the name, supplier and catalog
number of a commercial antibody.
For non-commercial antibodies either an [RRID](https://rrid.site) or the
host-animal and immunogen used (for examples: `"RRID:AB_2122563"` or
`"Rabbit anti-Human HTR5A Polyclonal Antibody, Invitrogen, Catalog # PA1-2453"`).
MAY be an array of strings if different antibodies are used in each channel of the file.

Field description: Description(s) of the secondary antibody used for immunostaining.
Either an [RRID](https://rrid.site) or the name, supplier and catalog
number of a commercial antibody.
For non-commercial antibodies either an [RRID](https://rrid.site) or the
host-animal and immunogen used (for examples: `"RRID:AB_228322"` or
`"Goat anti-Mouse IgM Secondary Antibody, Invitrogen, Catalog # 31172"`).

Field description: Text of the instructions given to participants before the recording.

Field description: [URI](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#uniform-resource-indicator)
of the corresponding [Cognitive Atlas](https://www.cognitiveatlas.org/)
Task term.

Field description: [URI](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#uniform-resource-indicator)
of the corresponding [CogPO](http://www.cogpo.org/) term.
Field description: Name of the cap manufacturer (for example, `"EasyCap"`).

Field description: Manufacturer's designation of the cap model
(for example, `"actiCAP 64 Ch Standard-2"`).
Field description: Number of miscellaneous channels not covered otherwise.

Field description: Defines whether the recording is `"continuous"`, `"discontinuous"`, or
`"epoched"`, where `"epoched"` is limited to time windows about events of
interest (for example, stimulus presentations or subject responses).

Field description: Description of the location of the ground electrode
(for example, `"placed on right mastoid (M2)"`).
Field description: Circumference of the participant's head, expressed in cm (for example, `58`).

Field description: Placement scheme of EEG electrodes.
Either the name of a standardized placement system (for example, `"10-20"`)
or a list of standardized electrode names (for example, `["Cz", "Pz"]`).

Field description: Object of temporal hardware filters applied, or `"n/a"` if the data is not
available. Each key-value pair in the JSON object is a name of the filter and
an object in which its parameters are defined as key-value pairs.
For example, `{"Highpass RC filter": {"Half amplitude cutoff (Hz)":
0.0159, "Roll-off": "6dB/Octave"}}`.

Field description: Freeform description of the observed subject artifact and its possible cause
(for example, `"Vagus Nerve Stimulator"`, `"non-removable implant"`).
If this field is set to `"n/a"`, it will be interpreted as absence of major
source of artifacts except cardiac and blinks.
Field description: Manufacturer of the equipment that produced the measurements.
Field description: Manufacturer's model name of the equipment that produced the measurements.

Field description: Manufacturer's designation of software version of the equipment that produced
the measurements.

Field description: The serial number of the equipment that produced the measurements.
A pseudonym can also be used to prevent the equipment from being
identifiable, so long as each pseudonym is unique within the dataset.
Field description: Text of the instructions given to participants before the recording.

of the corresponding [Cognitive Atlas](https://www.cognitiveatlas.org/)

Field description: Object containing key-value pairs related to the software used to present

(for example a tracking system in motion), can take values such as, "NaN", "0".

A pseudonym can also be used to prevent the equipment from being
identifiable, so long as each pseudonym is unique within the dataset.

Field description: Manufacturer's designation of software version of the equipment that produced
the measurements.
Field description: Text of the instructions given to participants before the recording.
Field description: Number of Latency channels.
Field description: Number of miscellaneous channels not covered otherwise.

Field description: Describes how missing values are represented in the given recording system
(for example a tracking system in motion), can take values such as, "NaN", "0".

Field description: Freeform description of the observed subject artifact and its possible cause
(for example, `"Vagus Nerve Stimulator"`, `"non-removable implant"`).
If this field is set to `"n/a"`, it will be interpreted as absence of major
source of artifacts except cardiac and blinks.

codeMessages.SIDECAR_FIELD_OVERRIDE: Sidecar files should not override values assigned at a higher level.
codeMessages.JSON_KEY_RECOMMENDED: A JSON file is missing a key listed as recommended.
codeMessages.SIDECAR_KEY_RECOMMENDED: A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
codeMessages.TSV_COLUMN_TYPE_REDEFINED: A column required in a TSV file has been redefined in a sidecar file. This redefinition is being ignored.
codeMessages.EOG_CHANNEL_COUNT_MISMATCH:
The EOGChannelCount metadata does not match the number of channels
with type EOG in the associated channels.tsv file.
codeMessages.MISC_CHANNEL_COUNT_MISMATCH:
The MiscChannelCount metadata does not match the number of channels
with type MISC in the associated channels.tsv file.
codeMessages.ECG_CHANNEL_COUNT_MISMATCH:
The ECGChannelCount metadata does not match the number of channels
with type ECG in the associated channels.tsv file.
codeMessages.EXCESSIVE_ELECTRODE_SPECIFICITY:
Task, acquisition or run entities detected in electrodes.tsv.
Electrode definitions should generally not vary within a session.
Consider removing the excess entity/entities or create a new
session each time electrodes are reconfigured.
codeMessages.EVENTS_TSV_MISSING:
Task scans should have a corresponding 'events.tsv' file.
If this is a resting state scan you can ignore this warning or rename the task to include the word "rest".
